title: Bangers and mash
subtitle: Pork sausages, buttery mash, and rich onion gravy
excerpt: Good pork sausages baked or fried until deeply browned, served on creamy mashed potato with a slow-cooked onion gravy. The three elements each need attention: properly browned sausages, well-seasoned buttery mash, and a gravy that is more than just stock with onions.
sourceNotes: Bangers and mash is one of the most enduring British comfort foods, served in pubs and homes in the same form for over a century. The 'banger' name comes from the tendency of sausages with too much water content to burst during cooking. The dish became popular in World War I and II when meat content in sausages was reduced and the water-filled casing did indeed bang. Quality butchers' sausages should not burst.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The onion gravy can be made 2 days ahead and reheated. It improves with time.

content.text: The onion gravy is the most important element. The onions must be cooked slowly until they are soft, sweet, and beginning to colour — at least 20 minutes. A rushed onion gravy made with stock cube and a quick fry tastes of nothing. The caramelised onions are where all the flavour comes from.

items.prepNote: good pork, ideally from a butcher

content.text: Melt the {{unsalted-butter}} in a pan over low-medium heat. Add the {{onion}} and {{thyme}}. Cook slowly for 20 to 25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the onions are deeply softened and beginning to colour. Stir in the {{plain-flour}} and cook for 2 minutes. Add the {{worcestershire-sauce}} and {{stock-beef}}. Simmer for 10 minutes until thickened. Season to taste.

Cook the {{sausages-pork}} in a frying pan over medium heat, turning regularly, for 15 to 20 minutes until deeply browned all over and cooked through. Meanwhile, boil the {{potato}} until completely tender. Drain and steam-dry. Mash with {{unsalted-butter}}, warm {{whole-milk}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} until smooth and creamy. Serve the sausages on the mash with the gravy poured over.

items.cause: Pan too hot, or turning too soon
items.fix: Cook on medium heat and do not move them until a crust has formed on each side. High heat causes the skin to split before the interior is cooked

items.cause: Insufficient flour, or not reduced enough after adding stock
items.fix: Allow the gravy to simmer for at least 10 minutes after adding the stock. If still thin, dissolve 1 teaspoon of plain flour in a little cold water and stir in

content.text: Bangers and mash is the standard British pub supper and one of the fastest weeknight meals in the domestic repertoire. The quality of the sausage determines the quality of the dish — a good butcher's sausage with a high meat content is a different product from a supermarket budget sausage.
